key_points: Roommates can be placed in your room mid-year without warning
Freshman dorms strongly recommended — biggest regret for those who skipped
Housing office slow to communicate roommate changes or dropouts
key_quotes: I met some great friends in the dorms that I still talk to 15+ years later.
I lived in an apartment instead of dorms. It's my biggest regret from college.
They are allowed to move a student into your room at any time.

key_points: Party culture has faded significantly from peak 2010-2014 era
FallFest — 30+ year tradition — officially dead after years of decline
Gameday drunk crowds genuinely rowdy; avoid campus McDonald's on game day
key_quotes: Are there parties? Yes. Is it a complete shitshow like the late 00s? Nah.
Moving it away from downtown was the kiss of death.
Win or lose, tradition demands upholstery sacrifice.

key_points: Sodexo fired; new provider Elior/WVU Hospitality Group now running dining
Students cautiously optimistic about dining improvements under new management
University Kroger nicknamed 'Kroghetto' — moldy bread found on shelves
key_quotes: Students are going to like the Dining Services MUCH better this year.
I found an entire loaf of molded bread once at Kroger.
Locals we call that Kroghetto.

key_points: Multiple student suicides reported; recurring location flagged but not addressed
Loneliness and social isolation a real problem for many students
Carruth Center exists but students often unaware or reluctant to use it
key_quotes: That's not the only suicide from the same place in the last ten years... they might want to see if they can do something preventative there.
I've been struggling with mental health. I don't have any good friends, no plans for breaks.
If you have been struggling, have you tried the Carruth Center? They offer a ton of services.

key_points: Merit scholarships generous and accessible for out-of-state students
Financial aid appeals process unclear; aid packages can disappoint high-need students
Grad students in English department received surprise $300 paycheck cuts mid-semester
key_quotes: I got back my financial aid offer from WVU and it's not at all what I was expecting. Despite having a -945 SAI, the only aid I got was a GPA-based scholarship.
Grad students that are teaching in the English department had to take a 300 dollar loss on their paychecks and they found out when they got their paycheck.
WVU will not be offering any tuition refunds to students.

key_points: Budget cuts gutted humanities; math and English programs lost faculty in 'Transformation'
Forensic science program widely praised as a legitimate draw
Some professors fail to post grades all semester; grade appeal process is brutal
key_quotes: I NEVER RECEIVED MY EXAM 1 GRADE TO THIS DAY PHYSICALLY OR VIRTUALLY.
The forensics program is well regarded and the department is strong.
What's the situation like now after the budget cuts in the English department? Grad students had to take a $300 loss on their paychecks and found out when they got their paycheck.

key_points: 'Transformation' cuts left faculty demoralized and departments unstable
Admissions office sent false acceptance emails to applicants — major error
A professor's fate was decided via a Qualtrics poll — no formal process
key_quotes: The qualtrics poll to determine a colleague's fate is one of the most WVU things I've heard.
She apologized profusely — it was a marketing scheme. Her application is still in review.
Gee leaves and kids start applying again.

key_points: PRT is iconic but breaks frequently and maxes out at semester start
Morgantown is far more livable than WV stereotypes suggest; close to Pittsburgh
Hills are real — campus is not flat and splits across two main areas
key_quotes: Parking is a joke, taking elevators is laughable, and the PRT is maxed and always broken.
Morgantown is diverse, an hour from Pittsburgh, and outside of rent, very cheap to live in.
There is literally no good solution — I'd love to close it to cars but the traffic on Beechurst will be nuts.

key_points: Forensics and engineering programs have solid reputations for career placement
WVU frequently cited as a safety school — perception affects employer signaling
Alumni network active nationally; WVU events in DC and other major cities
key_quotes: Will choosing WVU limit my potential for getting into a graduate school with a fair amount of prestige?
The forensics program is well regarded and the department is strong.
WVU isn't a school that gets much talk on here but every once in a while I see a negative comment.

key_points: Out-of-state sticker price is steep but merit scholarships can offset significantly
Strong value for in-state students; one of WV's only real university options
Paying out-of-state for accounting or humanities degrees questioned by current students
key_quotes: Why pay out of state tuition for an accounting degree the same as your local Ohio in-state programs?
WVU's scholarships seem very generous even for out of state.
I'm ineligible for loans and have no co-signer. I've been working Wendy's, budgeting tight.

key_points: Strong school spirit and community; 'Country Roads' culture is genuinely real
Students describe feeling 'at home' on campus but lonely in the crowd
Applications at 10-year high post-Gee era — momentum shifting positive
key_quotes: I feel so at home at WVU yet so out of place with the people.
I went on a tour of WVU yesterday — god I could see myself fitting in there easily.
About fucking time, was getting worried there for a sec.

key_points: Safety: multiple suicides at same campus garage; no preventative barriers installed
Racism: documented racial slurs from students; 'fix your skin color' incident on video
Greek Life: wrongful death suit settled for $3M; delayed 911 call during fraternity incident
Sexual Assault: students publicly accused administration of failing assault victims
key_quotes: That's not the only suicide from the same place in the last ten years... they might want to see if they can do something preventative there.
Unfortunately frat boys hurled slurs at staff. This BS has got to stop.
Another student in a campus-wide trend has called out the administration at WVU for not helping sexual assault victims.

key_points: Rifle team has won 21 national championships — dominant and underrated program
PRT is a genuinely unique transit system; 50 years old and still iconic
Morgantown surprisingly affordable and livable compared to peer college towns
key_quotes: I love that there's an NCAA-recognized sport that we absolutely dominate in.
The Pride of West Virginia! The Pride was the best thing to happen to me during college.
I was honestly surprised by how nice Morgantown is. Everything online made it seem like I'd be living in a dirt-poor area.
